metadata.purpose: Bend feature to Axon integration mapping with feasibility, impact, effort scores (1-5 scale)

recommendation.name: Implicit Parallelism
recommendation.bendFeature: Automatic parallelism via interaction-net reduction — all independent redexes fire concurrently without programmer annotation.
recommendation.axonAdaptation: MIR DAG analysis to detect independent tensor operations and dispatch them to parallel execution lanes automatically.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: MIR pass identifies independent tensor subgraphs and marks them for parallel dispatch.
Achieves at least 2x wall-clock scaling on 4 physical cores for embarrassingly parallel tensor workloads.
No correctness regression — sequential and parallel outputs match within floating-point epsilon.

recommendation.name: Fold/Bend Constructs
recommendation.bendFeature: Built-in fold (bottom-up) and bend (top-down) recursive combinators that the runtime parallelises over tree structures.
recommendation.axonAdaptation: Introduce fold/reduce as first-class parallel tensor operations that desugar into optimised MIR reduction nodes.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: fold keyword is tokenised, parsed into FoldExpr AST node, type-checked, and lowered to MIR.
fold desugars to a parallel reduction operation in the MIR pass pipeline.
Associative-operator fold produces identical results to sequential accumulation.

recommendation.name: Math Notation
recommendation.bendFeature: Bend's concise notation for numeric and recursive definitions inspired by mathematical syntax.
recommendation.axonAdaptation: Lightweight @math block enabling subscript/superscript-style indexing and summation notation inside Axon source.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Design document produced and reviewed — deferred to EPSILON phase; no code changes required.
Document specifies concrete syntax, scoping rules, and desugaring strategy.

recommendation.name: Accumulative Diagnostics
recommendation.bendFeature: Bend's compiler collects all errors in a single pass rather than aborting on first error.
recommendation.axonAdaptation: Configurable severity diagnostics engine with --warn, --deny, and --allow flags that accumulates all errors before reporting.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: All compilation errors from parsing, type-checking, and codegen are collected into a unified diagnostics list.
--warn, --deny, and --allow CLI flags control per-diagnostic-category severity.
Compilation no longer aborts on first error — all recoverable errors are reported in a single run.

recommendation.name: Transform Pass Architecture
recommendation.bendFeature: Bend's multi-pass transformation pipeline with ordered rewrite rules over interaction nets.
recommendation.axonAdaptation: New src/mir/transform/ module hierarchy with a PassManager that registers, orders, and executes MIR-to-MIR transform passes.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: src/mir/transform/ directory contains 5 or more pass files with a common Pass trait.
PassManager executes passes in a defined, configurable order.
Each pass is independently testable and can be enabled/disabled via CLI flags.

recommendation.name: ADT Encoding
recommendation.bendFeature: Bend encodes algebraic data types as Scott-encoded lambdas for interaction-net compatibility.
recommendation.axonAdaptation: Selectable ADT memory layout via --enum-layout flag (tagged, packed, or scattered) for performance tuning.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: --enum-layout flag accepted by CLI with at least two layout strategies implemented.
Generated LLVM IR correctly represents enum variants under each layout.
Round-trip tests verify construct/match correctness for all supported layouts.

recommendation.name: Linearity
recommendation.bendFeature: Bend enforces linearity constraints — each variable is used exactly once — enabling safe parallel reduction.
recommendation.axonAdaptation: Optional linear/unique type annotations with a borrow-checker-style pass that enforces single-use semantics for marked bindings.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: linear and unique annotations are parsed and represented in the AST.
Type checker enforces single-use semantics for linear-annotated bindings.
Violations produce clear diagnostic messages with source spans.
Zero-copy tensor transfer verified on at least 5 distinct patterns.

recommendation.name: Monadic IO
recommendation.bendFeature: Bend separates pure computation from IO effects using monadic-style sequencing.
recommendation.axonAdaptation: with/ask blocks for effect sequencing — desugars to monadic bind chains in the MIR.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: with and ask keywords are tokenised and parsed into WithBlock AST nodes.
Type checker validates monadic bind chains and infers effect types.
Desugaring to explicit bind calls is verified via MIR snapshot tests.

recommendation.name: Snapshot Testing
recommendation.bendFeature: Bend uses golden-file testing to catch regressions in compiled output across compiler changes.
recommendation.axonAdaptation: Insta-based snapshot testing infrastructure for AST, MIR, LLVM IR, and diagnostic output golden files.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: insta crate added as dev-dependency in Cargo.toml.
100 or more snapshot files covering AST, MIR, LLVM IR, and diagnostics output.
cargo insta review workflow documented and functional.

recommendation.name: Combinator Floating
recommendation.bendFeature: Bend's pre-reduction pass floats closed combinators to top-level definitions to maximize sharing and parallelism.
recommendation.axonAdaptation: MIR pass that extracts closed subexpressions (no free variables) into top-level named combinators for reuse and parallel evaluation.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Closed subexpressions are identified and hoisted to module-level MIR definitions.
Floating does not change program semantics — verified by differential testing against unfloated output.
Inline pass can selectively re-inline floated combinators when beneficial for locality.

recommendation.name: Global Interning
recommendation.bendFeature: Bend interns all names and labels globally for O(1) comparison throughout the compiler pipeline.
recommendation.axonAdaptation: Unified NameInterner replacing ad-hoc string handling with interned symbols for O(1) equality and reduced memory usage.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: NameInterner provides insert and lookup with O(1) equality comparison via integer IDs.
All identifier comparisons in parser and AST use interned symbols instead of string equality.
Memory usage for name storage measurably reduced on large input programs.

recommendation.name: Comprehensions
recommendation.bendFeature: Bend supports list and tree comprehension syntax for declarative collection construction.
recommendation.axonAdaptation: Python-style comprehension syntax [expr for x in iter] that desugars to map/filter tensor operations.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Comprehension syntax is tokenised, parsed into ComprehensionExpr AST node, and type-checked.
Desugaring to map/filter calls is verified via MIR snapshot tests.
Nested comprehensions and guard clauses are supported.

recommendation.name: GPU Branching
recommendation.bendFeature: Bend's interaction-net model avoids branching, which maps well to GPU SIMT execution.
recommendation.axonAdaptation: MIR branchless transforms that convert conditional tensor operations into predicated select instructions for GPU targets.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Design document produced specifying branchless transform rules and GPU target constraints — deferred; no code changes required.
Document covers select-instruction lowering, divergence analysis, and warp-efficiency metrics.

recommendation.name: Stack Safety
recommendation.bendFeature: Bend's runtime uses trampolining and stack management to handle deep recursion safely.
recommendation.axonAdaptation: Integrate stacker crate for segmented-stack overflow protection in deeply recursive compiler phases.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: stacker crate added as dependency in Cargo.toml.
Recursive descent in parser, type checker, and MIR builder wrapped with stacker::maybe_grow.
Deeply nested input (1000+ levels) no longer causes stack overflow.

recommendation.name: Operator Fusion
recommendation.bendFeature: Bend fuses adjacent interaction-net rules to reduce allocation and improve cache locality.
recommendation.axonAdaptation: MIR pass that fuses chains of element-wise tensor operations into single kernels, eliminating intermediate allocations.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Chains of 2+ element-wise tensor ops are fused into single loop bodies in generated code.
Intermediate tensor allocations are eliminated for fused chains.
Achieves at least 3x throughput improvement on representative fusion benchmarks.
Fused output matches unfused output within floating-point epsilon for all test cases.

recommendation.name: Granular CLI Flags
recommendation.bendFeature: Bend exposes per-pass optimization flags for fine-grained compiler behavior control.
recommendation.axonAdaptation: Per-pass -O flags (e.g., -Ofuse, -Ofloat, -Oparallel) to enable/disable individual MIR transform passes.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: CLI accepts -O{passname} flags to toggle individual transform passes.
--help output documents all available pass flags.
Disabled passes are skipped by the PassManager without side effects.
